reasons.title: Prime Growth Location
reasons.description: Strategically located within an hour and a half of 5 million people in Buckeye, one of America's fastest-growing cities.

reasons.title: Multi-Asset Resort Model
reasons.description: Combines a state-of-the-art surf lagoon with hotel lodging, RV glamping, and world-class bass fishing to create multiple synergistic revenue streams.

reasons.title: Covered Land Strategy
reasons.description: The existing RV, lake, and event operations provide immediate value and income, acting as a protected 'covered land play' during the surf park construction phase.

summary.quote: Hidden Lake is a world-class surf and recreation destination that combines real operating performance with long-term community impact.
paragraphs: Hidden Lake Surf Resort is a 120-acre lakefront development that will host proven cash flow generators in the densely populated Phoenix Metropolitan area. The resort will feature a state-of-the-art Surf Park, Beach Club, pools, surf shop, and F&B, alongside 300-room hotel lodging, RV camping, glamping, and overwater casitas. The site also includes world-class bass fishing, boating, and a surfside amphitheater for concerts and corporate events.

The project site is in the rapidly expanding City of Buckeye, Arizona, which is currently the largest city in the state by land area and a top-five national destination for RV travel. The Sponsor has already invested $6.5 million of cash equity and secured vital entitlements, water rights, and power access—critical barriers to entry that have historically hindered similar surf park developments in the United States.
summary.conclusion: With land, water rights, and municipal approvals already secured, the project is positioned to capture massive demand in the underserved Southwest surf market.

cards.title: Financial Returns Orientation
cards.summary: Targeting superior risk-adjusted returns through a combination of high-margin surf passes, hospitality revenue, and strategic residential land sales.

cards.title: Property Overview
cards.summary: A massive mixed-use destination anchored by a 60-acre lake and a heart-shaped surf lagoon capable of producing up to 700 waves per hour.

cards.title: Market Analysis
cards.summary: Buckeye is a hub for innovation and growth, featuring major investments like Bill Gates' Belmont Smart City and Howard Hughes' 34,000-acre Teravalis community.

cards.title: Sponsor Profile
cards.summary: Led by Drew Boyles, a veteran entrepreneur and former Mayor, the team brings decades of experience in large-scale real estate development and operations.

newsLinks.title: Endless Surf Technology Overview
newsLinks.description: Website for the pneumatic wave technology provider selected for the Hidden Lake project.

timeline.description: Focus on site approvals, entitlements, and infrastructure design.
Early revenue from land sales ($18.4M) and RV/Lake operations.

benefits.title: Capital Gains Deferral
benefits.description: Investors with 2025 capital gains may qualify for tax deferral.

benefits.title: Depreciation Deductions
benefits.description: Investment qualifies for income tax deductions via project depreciation.

benefits.title: Zero Future Capital Gains
benefits.description: Project is located in an Opportunity Zone, offering potential for zero future capital gains tax on appreciation.

saleWaterfall.description: Return of initial capital to all equity partners.
LP investors pay carried interest to Sponsor upon achieving success hurdles; Seed investors are exempt.

cashFlowDistribution.description: Seed Round investors receive ownership percentage equal to investment relative to total equity.
Seed Round investors do not pay incentive compensation (promote) to the Sponsor.

facts.description: Capacity across the resort hotel component

facts.description: Size of the onsite lake fed by the Gila River

specialFeatures.title: World-Class Surf & Resort Finishes
specialFeatures.description: Resort features Endless Surf pneumatic wave technology capable of 400-700 waves per hour. Lodging includes a mix of traditional hotel rooms, overwater casitas, glamping tents, and villas. Interior finishes are designed for a luxury desert destination.

highlights.title: Rapid Economic Growth
highlights.description: Consistently ranks as one of the fastest-growing cities in the United States since 2017.

highlights.title: Densely Populated Reach
highlights.description: Located within a 1.5-hour drive of approximately 5.1 million people in the Phoenix MSA.

highlights.title: Outdoor Recreation Synergy
highlights.description: Adjacent to federally managed BLM land with a connected trail system and Gila River access.

timeline.title: Entitlements & Zoning
timeline.description: Site acquisition, zoning, and PAD approval from the City of Buckeye completed.

timeline.title: Seed Round Offering
timeline.description: Funding for land interest and final predevelopment steps.

timeline.title: Project Groundbreaking (Q1 2027)
timeline.description: Anticipated start for surf lagoon and hospitality improvements.

timeline.title: Resort Opening (Q1 2029)
timeline.description: Full opening of surf, beach, and hotel operations.

phases.features: Land interest, predevelopment, and infrastructure planning.
Construction of RV sites, lake improvements, and amenities.
Lagoon, Hotel, and Beach Club construction and opening.

drivers.title: Tech & Data Hub Expansion
drivers.description: Development of the 'Buckeye Tech Corridor' includes a $20 billion master-planned data center complex across 2,000 acres for hyperscale operators.

drivers.title: Master-Planned Growth
drivers.description: The Teravalis and Verrado master-planned communities are bringing over 100,000 homes and millions of square feet of commercial space to the immediate submarket.

drivers.title: Smart City Infrastructure
drivers.description: Investment in the 25,000-acre 'Belmont' Smart City, designed to integrate autonomous vehicles and high-speed digital networks.

drivers.title: Tourism & RV Demand
drivers.description: Phoenix is ranked as a top 5 RV destination nationally, with the West Valley specifically identified as underserved for high-quality hospitality and recreation.

analysis.title: Rapid Population Growth
analysis.description: Phoenix is one of the fastest-growing cities in the U.S. since 2017, leading to a surge in demand for regional recreational destinations.

analysis.title: Underserved Submarket
analysis.description: The West Valley of Arizona is currently underserved with only a handful of dated RV parks and limited high-end experiential hospitality.

analysis.title: High Barriers to Entry
analysis.description: Securing surf park sites with necessary water, power, and municipal approvals is a significant barrier to entry, limiting future competition.

paragraphs: Endless Pursuit Corporation, led by US Navy Veteran and former Mayor Drew Boyles, is developing the Hidden Lake Surf Resort. This 120-acre project in Buckeye, Arizona, features a 60-acre lake and 60 acres of land designed to host a state-of-the-art surf park, 300-room hotel, and extensive recreational facilities.

The Sponsor has already invested $6.5 million of cash equity into the project. The development leverages a 'covered land play' strategy, using existing fishing and event revenue to mitigate risk while the world-class surf technology and resort infrastructure are constructed.

teamMembers.name: Drew Boyles
teamMembers.title: CEO & Founder
teamMembers.background: US Navy Veteran and USC MBA graduate with over 20 years experience building service companies. Former Mayor of El Segundo.

teamMembers.name: Jeff Zuckerman
teamMembers.title: Real Estate Investment Strategy
teamMembers.background: Expert in institutional real estate investment who has led platforms managing $4.5B+ for firms like AEW Capital Management.

teamMembers.name: Peter Solar
teamMembers.title: Construction & Development Leadership
teamMembers.background: Has developed over $3 billion in ground-up construction for major firms including Alliance Residential and Trammell Crow.

partners.name: WhiteWater / Endless Surf
partners.description: World-renowned water park developer providing the Endless Surf wave technology.

partners.name: City of Buckeye
partners.description: Approved the Planned Area Development and negotiating GPLET tax incentives.

partners.name: Bureau of Land Management (BLM)
partners.description: Managing the adjacent federally owned land and trail system.

advantages.title: Civic & Economic Expertise
advantages.description: Founder Drew Boyles is a former Mayor and management consultant with deep experience in public-private partnerships and economic development.

advantages.title: Institutional Discipline
advantages.description: The leadership team has managed over $4.5B in institutional capital and developed $3B+ in ground-up construction projects.

advantages.title: Significant Skin in the Game
advantages.description: The Sponsor has already committed $6.5 million in cash equity, aligning their interests directly with seed-round investors.

advantages.title: Preferential Fee Structure
advantages.description: Unlike the Limited Partners, Seed Round investors do not pay a 'promote' or incentive compensation to the Sponsor.
